title: Method and apparatus for allocating ptrs in wireless communication system
abstract: A communication method and system for converging a 5 th -Generation (5G) communication system for supporting higher data rates beyond a 4 th -Generation (4G) system with a technology for internet of things (IoT) is provided. The communication method includes applying to intelligent services based on the 5G communication technology and the IoT-related technology, such as smart home, smart building, smart city, smart car, connected car, health care, digital education, smart retail, security and safety services. The disclosure relates to a method and an apparatus for allocating a phase tracking reference signal (PTRS) for estimating and compensating for phase distortion due to phase noise, Doppler effect, or synchronization error.
claims: Claims ( 15 ) A method by a terminal for receiving a reference signal in a wireless communication system, the method comprising: receiving, from a base station, information on a resource location of a phase tracking reference signal (PTRS); receiving the PTRS at a location on a frequency axis identified based on the information on the resource location; and performing phase tracking using the PTRS. The method of claim 1, wherein the location on the frequency axis on which the PTRS is transmitted is determined based on a virtual resource block (VRB) index, and wherein the PTRS is allocated at a predetermined interval on the VRB index. The method of claim 1, wherein a port of the PTRS corresponds to any one of a plurality of demodulation reference signal (DMRS) ports, and wherein the DMRS port to which the PTRS port is mapped is indicated by the information on the resource location. The method of claim 1, wherein the information on the resource location is information indicating an offset of a resource element (RE) to which the PTRS is mapped, and wherein the information on the resource location is received through radio resource control (RRC) signaling. A terminal receiving a reference signal in a wireless communication system, the terminal comprising: a transceiver configured to transmit and receive signals; and at least one processor configured to: receive from a base station information on a resource location of a phase tracking reference signal (PTRS), receive the PTRS at a location on a frequency axis identified based on the information on the resource location, and perform phase tracking using the PTRS. The terminal of claim 5, wherein the location on the frequency axis on which the PTRS is transmitted is determined based on a virtual resource block (VRB) index, and wherein the PTRS is allocated at a predetermined interval on the VRB index. The terminal of claim 5, wherein a port of the PTRS corresponds to any one of a plurality of demodulation reference signal (DMRS) ports, and wherein the DMRS port to which the PTRS port is mapped is indicated by the information on the resource location. The terminal of claim 5, wherein the information on the resource location is information indicating an offset of a resource element (RE) to which the PTRS is mapped, and wherein the information on the resource location is received through radio resource control (RRC) signaling. A method by a base station for transmitting a reference signal in a wireless communication system, the method comprising: determining a resource location of a phase tracking reference signal (PTRS) to be transmitted, to a terminal; transmitting information on the resource location to the terminal; and transmitting, to the terminal, the PTRS for performing phase tracking at a location on a frequency axis identified based on the information on the resource location. The method of claim 9, wherein the location on the frequency axis on which the PTRS is transmitted is determined based on a virtual resource block (VRB) index, and wherein the PTRS is allocated at a predetermined interval on the VRB index. The method of claim 9, wherein a port of the PTRS corresponds to any one of a plurality of demodulation reference signal (DMRS) ports, wherein the DMRS port to which the PTRS port is mapped is indicated by the information on the resource location, wherein the information on the resource location is information indicating an offset of a resource element (RE) to which the PTRS is mapped, and wherein the information on the resource location is received through radio resource control (RRC) signaling. A base station transmitting a reference signal in a wireless communication system, the base station comprising: a transceiver configured to transmit/receive signals; and at least one processor configured to: determine a resource location of a phase tracking reference signal (PTRS) to be transmitted to a terminal, transmit information on the resource location to the terminal, and transmit, to the terminal, the PTRS for performing phase tracking at a location on a frequency axis identified based on the information on the resource location. The base station of claim 12, wherein the location on the frequency axis on which the PTRS is transmitted is determined based on a virtual resource block (VRB) index, and wherein the PTRS is allocated at a predetermined interval on the VRB index. The base station of claim 12, wherein a port of the PTRS corresponds to any one of a plurality of demodulation reference signal (DMRS) ports, and wherein the DMRS port to which the PTRS port is mapped is indicated by the information on the resource location. The base station of claim 12, wherein the information on the resource location is information indicating an offset of a resource element (RE) to which the PTRS is mapped, and wherein the information on the resource location is received through radio resource control (RRC) signaling.
